title: Card weaving: four-hole cards
subtitle: Weaving a warp-pattern band using four-hole card sequences and forward-backward turning
excerpt: Card weaving uses a deck of square cards with holes near the corners as the shed-making mechanism. Rotating the cards forward or backward creates the shed for each pick. The most common entry point is a four-hole card with a plain threaded warp, producing a simple warp-pattern band. This guide covers threading, setting up a backstrap or fixed tension, and weaving a basic forward-backward pattern.
sourceNotes: Hooper, Luther. 'Hand-Loom Weaving.' Pitman, 1910. Chapter on tablet weaving and card weaving. Internet Archive: archive.org/details/handloomweaving00hoop. Threading notation and forward-backward sequences are standard guild practice.

content.text: Card weaving produces narrow warp-pattern bands: belts, straps, borders, and trim. The
is created by rotating the card deck. A
of four-and-four (four turns forward, four turns back) produces a simple two-colour diagonal pattern and keeps the warp manageable.

items.name: Square weaving cards (4-hole), 8-16 cards for a starter project
Weaving warp yarn (cotton, wool, or linen at DK-worsted weight), 2 colours
Weft yarn (same as warp or contrasting)
Tapestry shuttle or small flat stick
Fixed tension point (belt anchor, loom bar, or clamp)

content.text: Thread each card with four warp ends, one through each hole (A, B, C, D from top-left clockwise). For a two-colour band, thread colour 1 through holes A and B, colour 2 through holes C and D. Thread all cards from the same direction (front to back). Tie the warp ends together at both ends and attach one end to a fixed point. Sit back to tension the warp against the fixed point.
Step 2: Open the first shed and pass the weft

Hold the card deck in your dominant hand and rotate all cards one quarter-turn forward (away from you). The shed opens: a gap appears between the upper and lower warp threads. Pass the weft through the shed and beat it toward the body with the edge of the shuttle.

Turn forward for picks 1, 2, 3, and 4: four quarter-turns forward total, passing the weft after each. Then turn backward for picks 5, 6, 7, and 8: four quarter-turns backward. This is one complete four-and-four cycle. The twist in the warp threads reverses on the backward passes, preventing the warp from locking up. Repeat the cycle.
Step 4: Advance the band and finish

As the woven band grows, slide the cards toward the fixed end to advance the weaving. Beat each pick firmly to produce an even, firm band. When finished, tie off both ends and trim the fringe to a consistent length.

items.fix: The four-and-four sequence reverses the twist reliably. If weaving a longer forward sequence for pattern, plan additional reverse passes to un-twist.
items.cause: Too many turns in one direction without reversing
items.problem: The warp threads twist together and the shed closes

items.fix: Pass the weft in a slight arc and beat with consistent pressure. Maintain the same amount of weft in each shed.
items.cause: Weft tension varying: too loose produces a wide band, too tight narrows it
items.problem: The band is wider at one end than the other

items.fix: Thread half the cards with colour 1 in holes A-B and colour 2 in C-D; thread the other half with colour 1 in C-D and colour 2 in A-B. The alternation produces the diagonal.
items.cause: All cards threaded in the same rotation direction AND with the same colour arrangement
items.problem: The pattern looks the same in both directions and produces no diagonal
